assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: Investigation.md lines 104-111 contain a summary table listing two root causes: (1) 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform -- lowercases and kebab-cases headings instead of preserving title case' in 'shared/convention-utils.md', and (2) 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of Task issue type ID (10050) -- reads wrong config field' in 'plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a'. These are identified across Steps 2-4 (Code-Path Tracing at lines 62-100, Codebase Investigation at lines 88-100, Root Cause Analysis at lines 102-121).

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: Investigation.md line 122: 'This triggers the **Decomposition Guard** (Step 6 of triage-bug).' Lines 115-120 detail independence: 'Different modules: Root cause 1 is in shared/convention-utils.md (a shared utility), while root cause 2 is in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a' and 'Different code paths: Root cause 1 is triggered during convention-aware task enrichment (Step 5), while root cause 2 is triggered during Jira task creation (Step 6a).' Decomposition-guard.md lines 9-11 restate: 'Root cause 1 is in the shared convention utilities (shared/convention-utils.md)... Root cause 2 is in the plan-feature skill (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a).'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: Decomposition-guard.md lines 5-7: Issue 1 presented as 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' with description of the kebab-case bug. Issue 2 presented as 'Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' with description of the Feature vs Task ID bug. Lines 9-11 further specify modules and code paths for each root cause.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: Decomposition-guard.md lines 13-17: 'Options: 1. **Proceed** -- create a single Task covering both fixes 2. **Split** -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually Choose (1/2):'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: Decomposition-guard.md ends at line 17 with 'Choose (1/2):' followed by an empty line 18. No further output exists -- no task creation, no Jira API calls, no continuation beyond the prompt. The only output files are investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md; no task creation artifacts are present. The skill explicitly stops and presents a choice, waiting for user input before proceeding.
